matrix.requirement: Concurrent AppendEdge landing inside Compact's read-then-truncate window for the same node must not be lost
The append must eventually be merged into graph.dat (not just 'not deleted')
matrix.test: TestCompactConcurrentAppendNotLost (new) - second Compact call asserts merged weight
matrix.covers: Test spec's 'eventually merged, not silently dropped'

matrix.requirement: No data race between AppendEdge and Compact's read/truncate paths
matrix.covers: Race-detector confidence, not just logical correctness

matrix.requirement: Fix must not regress prior 4.5.11.1 fix (segment floor / no-reuse) or retry-idempotency (sidecar)
Standalone (non-Compact) callers of TruncateNode/ReadNodeAfter/ReadNode remain unaffected
matrix.covers: Backward compatibility of unchanged call sites

matrix.requirement: No deadlock introduced by new per-node lock
matrix.test: Full engine/graph + engine module suite under -race, count=2-3, plus the new race test itself exercising the lock under real goroutine contention
matrix.covers: Deadlock-risk mitigation called out in the subtask brief

matrix.requirement: No regression across the rest of the engine module (btree, mvcc, wal, split, rpc, catalog)
